title: Zoro Cuts Through the Pressure
description: On pages 12–15, zoro cuts through the pressure focuses on Zoro, Hatchan, Usopp, Nami as zoro’s injuries worsen during the fight, but he continues battling Hatchan while the others struggle around him.
scene: On pages 12–15, zoro cuts through the pressure focuses on Zoro, Hatchan, Usopp, Nami as zoro’s injuries worsen during the fight, but he continues battling Hatchan while the others struggle around him.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Hey, yesaku... do you think brother's wounds might have reopened from that earlier attack!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: that bastard spun himself on top of my swords,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: And even ended up cutting my hands!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I really really really can't forgive you anymore! It's time to finish you off!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: He looks like he's hurting real bad...!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: If it were possible, I wish we could switch places right now!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Even if these wounds are normally enough to make me faint,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I can't afford, to pass out now.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: OCIOPUS POT STANCE! time to die!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Even if these wounds are normally enough to kill me,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: In order to beat that abnormally strong Hawkeys,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I can't afford to be normal either!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: now do you understand the difference in the weight of our swords?

summary: This part pushes the Arlong Park battle forward, clarifying who is fighting, what is at stake, and why Nami’s freedom matters.
